Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. LLM Architecture Gallery 478 upvotes, 37 comments
  2. Why Are Viral Capsids Icosahedral? 29 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. MoD sources warn Palantir role at heart of government is threat to UK security 28 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Reviewing Large Changes with Jujutsu 43 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 132 upvotes, 22 comments
  6. The Accidental Room (2018) 45 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 117 upvotes, 20 comments
  8. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance 839 upvotes, 244 comments
  9. What every computer scientist should know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 93 upvotes, 21 comments
  10. I Love FreeBSD 20 upvotes, 2 comments
  11. Six ingenious ways how Canon DSLRs used to illuminate their autofocus points 70 upvotes, 18 comments
  12. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 526 upvotes, 210 comments
  13. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant 20 upvotes, 3 comments
  14. Comparing Python Type Checkers: Typing Spec Conformance 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Stop Sloppypasta 471 upvotes, 192 comments
  16. The 49MB web page 671 upvotes, 299 comments
  17. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 176 upvotes, 68 comments
  18. Home Assistant waters my plants 103 upvotes, 42 comments
  19. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 321 upvotes, 181 comments
  20. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 394 upvotes, 237 comments
  21. Obsession with growth is destroying nature, 150 countries warn 22 upvotes, 7 comments
  22. LLMs can be exhausting 269 upvotes, 184 comments
  23. Bringing Semiconductors to Kazakhstan 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. How I write software with LLMs 326 upvotes, 269 comments
  25. Lazycut: A simple terminal video trimmer using FFmpeg 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. Simple, fast and open-source PHP framework frozen in C-extension 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. AirPods Max 2 41 upvotes, 38 comments
  28. Even Faster Asin() Was Staring Right at Me 12 upvotes, 7 comments
  29. Show HN: Hackerbrief – Top posts on Hacker News summarized daily 18 upvotes, 14 comments
  30. Nango (YC W23, API Access for Agents and Apps) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
